zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire於1456年出生在英國賀福爾時，他的父親Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys30歲，母親Margaret Maud VAUGHAN39歲。他於1489年與Alice Dycons在英國林可爾史爾斯坦福結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1494年住在英國林可爾史爾斯坦福。他在英國林可爾史爾斯坦福被列為welsh landowner。他去世於1541年9月14日在英國林可爾史爾享年85歲，埋葬在英國林可爾史爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire于1456年出生在英国賀福爾时，他的父亲Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys30岁，母亲Margaret Maud VAUGHAN39岁。他于1489年与Alice Dycons在英国林可爾史爾斯坦福结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1494年住在英国林可爾史爾斯坦福。他在英国林可爾史爾斯坦福被列为welsh landowner。他去世于1541年9月14日在英国林可爾史爾享年85岁，埋葬在英国林可爾史爾。

nameMeanings.name: Cecil,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Old Welsh personal name Seisyllt, sometimes wrongly said to derive from the Latin name Caecilius (see Sisley ). The most frequent modern spelling is a Renaissance attempt to connect the surname with Caecilius in the English pronunciation of its time. The Welsh name is more probably, but not definitely, from the Latin name Sextilius, a derivative of sextus ‘sixth’.

History: The great and powerful English Cecil family first came to prominence with David Cecil, a Monmouthshire gentleman who espoused the cause of Henry Tudor and came to court in London after the latter became king in 1485. His grandson William Cecil, Lord Burghley (1520–98), was Elizabeth I's chief adviser for 40 years, and his descendants have remained politically powerful and culturally influential in Britain ever since. They were originally minor Welsh gentry; their name is found in a variety of forms, including Sitsylt, Ceyssel, and Sisseld.

variants.name: Cissell
meanings: English (Middlesex): variant of Welsh   Cecil  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Sisley
meanings: English (Kent): from the popular Middle English female personal name Sisely (Old French Cecile, Cicile, from Latin Caecilia, feminine form of the Roman surname Caecilius, a derivative of caecus ‘blind’). This was the name of a early Christian virgin martyr of the 2nd or 3rd century, who came to be regarded as the patron saint of music. The surname is generally spelt with C- in the Middle Ages, following the trend of the personal name, and with S- in modern times. Compare Cecil and see also Sise , Sisson .

variants.name: Cissel
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Cecil  .

nameMeanings.name: High
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hay
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: Hy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heng, Hong, Sang, Dong, Khin, Kin, Kong, Man, Sin, Yong.  Vietnamese Soi, Cong, Dang, Dau, Duoc, Mui, Nhi, Phu, Quyen, Tau.
English: variant of   High  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 解, based on its pronunciation in Taishanese, a variant of Cantonese, see   Xie   2.

nameMeanings.name: Sheriff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name or nickname from Middle English shirreve, sher(r)eve, sheref(fe) ‘sheriff, officer of the Crown’ (Old English scīrgerēfa, from scīr ‘shire, county’ + (ge)rēfa ‘reeve’), literally the reeve (i.e. steward) of a shire. Compare Reeve .
Muslim (mainly Nigeria, Sierra Leone, and Liberia): variant of   Sharif  . In Sierra Leone and Liberia this name is found mainly among the Mandinka people. Compare   Sirleaf  .

variants.name: Sirleaf
meanings: West African (Liberia): altered form of the predominantly Mandinka Muslim name   Sheriff  .

variants.name: Reeve
meanings: English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English reve, refe ‘reeve’ (Old English gerēfa). In medieval England a reeve was an official responsible for the administration of a manor, including organizing work done by the peasants on the land for their lord, collecting rents, selling produce, and so on.
Irish: variant of   Reeves   5.

variants.name: Shreve
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Sheriff  . Compare   Shreeve  ,   Shreves  .

variants.name: Sherriff
meanings: English: variant of   Sheriff  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire was born in 1456, in Herefordshire, England, his father, Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys, was 30 and his mother, Margaret Maud VAUGHAN, was 39. He married Alice Dycons in 1489, in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He lived in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England in 1494. His occupation is listed as welsh landowner in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England. He died on 14 September 1541, in Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 85, and was buried in Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire est né en 1456, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Margaret Maud VAUGHAN, avait 39 ans. Il a épousé Alice Dycons en 1489, à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il a vécu à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre en 1494. Sa profession de son indiquée est welsh landowner à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Il est décédé le 14 septembre 1541, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 85 ans, et a été enterré à Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

Er hat 1489 in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England Alice Dycons geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1494 hat er in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England gelebt. sein Beruf ist als welsh landowner in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England aufgeführt. Er starb am 14. September 1541 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Lincolnshire, England begraben.

Lui sposato Alice Dycons nel 1489, nel Stamford, Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Vissuto a Stamford, Lincolnshire, England nel 1494. ja.lifeSummary: Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshireが1456年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynysは30歳，母Margaret Maud VAUGHANは39歳であった。1489年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードでAlice Dyconsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1494年にはイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードに住んでいた。イギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードでの職業はwelsh landownerと記録されている。彼は1541年9月14日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで85歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire(이)가 1456년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Maud VAUGHAN(은)는 39세였다. 그는 1489년 영국 링코실르 스타퍼드에서 Alice Dycons(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1494년도에 영국 링코실르 스타퍼드에서 살았다. 그의 직업은 영국 링코실르 스타퍼드에서 welsh landowner이었다. 그는 1541년 9월 14일 영국 링코실르에서 85세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire nasceu em 1456, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Maud VAUGHAN, tinha 39 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Dycons em 1489, em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele viveu em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra em 1494. Sua profissão aparece como welsh landowner em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 14 de setembro de 1541, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 85 anos, e foi sepultado em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Witham on the Hill, Lincolnshire, Englan: витам он т хилл, линколншир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire родился в 1456 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Maud VAUGHAN, 39. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Dycons в 1489 г. в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он жил в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия в 1494 году.  Он работал по профессии welsh landowner в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия. Он умер 14 сентября 1541 г. в местности линколншир, Англия в возрасте 85 лет и был похоронен в местности линколншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire nació en 1456, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Cecil ap Philip SEISYLLT of Alt-yr-Ynys, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Margaret Maud VAUGHAN, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Alice Dycons en 1489, en Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Vivió en Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra en 1494. Su ocupación registrada era welsh landowner en Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Murió el 14 de septiembre de 1541, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 85 años, y fue sepultado en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.
